content.title: © 2026 IntegrateWise LLP. Governed public projection of the Adaptive Spine and Knowledge Workspace.
What Is IntegrateWise
content.introduction: IntegrateWise maintains organizational continuity. It connects the tools where work already happens, stores truth and context in the Adaptive Spine, and exposes them through the Knowledge Workspace and the Twin. Every signal from every system feeds into one place. That place can think. You approve every move.

content.content:
The Spine was born on the floor of Salesforce, where Nirmal spent 13 years as a MuleSoft Integration Architect. Every day, the same ritual: open one system, check another, cross-reference a third, mentally stitch together the picture. The tools were excellent. None of them talked to each other. And someone had to bridge the gap.

That someone was the Human API. That someone built the Spine.

The Spine came first. Everything else followed.

IntegrateWise is the system that understands, remembers, reasons, and prepares execution — while execution happens in the customer's own environment.

We do not compete with workflow engines or automation platforms. We compete with the chaos of fragmented tools, disconnected context, and blind operational decisions.

The wedge: Every enterprise has 15+ tools. None of them talk to each other. The human operator becomes the integration layer. IntegrateWise replaces that burden with a unified intelligence layer that connects, unifies, understands, remembers, reasons, governs, prepares, and hands off.

IntegrateWise exposes five customer surfaces.

content.title: Two Modes
content.content:
Customer Zero Mode: Full stack (L0-L7) for IntegrateWise's internal operations and demos.

External Customer Mode: Trimmed to 5 surfaces + Handoff Layer (L1 Workbench, L2 Cognitive Overlay, L3 Twin Workbench, L4 Memory Workbench, Approval Center).

L1 Workbench — Main domain-specific operational surface (Knowledge Workspace)
L2 Cognitive Overlay — Always-available intelligence layer (signals, insights, context)
L3 Twin Workbench — Conversational reasoning and planning (proposes only)
L4 Memory Workbench — Knowledge substrate (search, memory, documents)
Approval Center — Governance surface (review, approve, dispatch — the HARD GATE)

L5 (Operations) and L6 (Execution) are Customer Zero only. Customers receive a Handoff Layer instead of native execution.

The operating loop:

Connect → Contextualize → Think → Approve → Handoff → Learn

1. Connect — Tools connect via the Continuity Bridge (MCP primary).
2. Contextualize — Context linked to entities in the Spine.
3. Think — The Twin generates proposals with evidence (proposes only).
4. Approve — Review in the Approval Center. Decisions teach the system.
5. Handoff — Prepares canonical contract and dispatches to your execution environment.
6. Learn — Outcomes feed back into the Adaptive Spine and Digital Memory.

The Adaptive Spine is the foundational layer. It connects source systems via the Continuity Bridge, extracts truth and context, organizes them into one usable structure, and stores the result so the Knowledge Workspace and Twin can read from one grounded view.

It is adaptive by design — starts universal and extends based on industry and department.

content.title: The Turning Point
content.introduction: How fragmented tools and the Human API problem led to the Adaptive Spine.

content.content:
For years, organizations lived with a fundamental mismatch: powerful specialized tools that cannot talk to each other, and humans acting as the glue.

This created the Human API problem at massive scale. IntegrateWise was built to eliminate that burden.

content.title: What the Spine Is
content.introduction: The Adaptive Spine is foundational infrastructure (S0) that powers the entire Knowledge Workspace.

content.content:
The Adaptive Spine is the single source of truth for your organization's entities, relationships, and context.

It is invisible to end users but powers every surface: the L1 Workbench, L2 Cognitive Overlay, L3 Twin Workbench, L4 Memory, and the Approval Center (HARD GATE).

MCP is the primary protocol boundary. The Continuity Bridge is how the Twin and external AIs reach prepared context — never direct tool reads.

The Knowledge Workspace (L1) is where operational work happens. Domain views converge on Entity 360, giving a complete picture of every account, opportunity, ticket or custom entity — with verified truth from every connected system plus context from the Twin and Digital Memory.

content.title: The Twin
content.introduction: Bounded reasoning that proposes. You govern and approve.

content.content:
The Twin reads exclusively through the Continuity Bridge over prepared context from the Adaptive Spine + Digital Memory. It never has direct access to your tools.

It generates proposals, evidence, and plans. Approval always happens in the Approval Center (the HARD GATE).

content.title: Twin Rule
content.content: Twin proposes only. Humans (or governed processes) approve and act via the Handoff Layer.

content.title: Connectors Overview
content.introduction: Connectors feed the Adaptive Spine via the Continuity Bridge (MCP primary).

content.content: Every input to the Knowledge Workspace enters through a connector. The Spine doesn't care where data comes from. It cares about what it means and how it relates to your entities.

content.title: Solutions Overview
content.introduction: Role-specific expressions of the same Knowledge Workspace.

content.content: Solutions for Account Success, Sales Ops, Business Ops, Finance Ops and Personal Ops all run on the shared Adaptive Spine, Continuity Bridge, and customer surfaces. Only the lens and primary workflows change per role and industry.

content.title: Glossary
content.introduction: Key terms aligned to the v3.7 External Customer Surface Lock.

content.content: The foundational layer that unifies truth and context from all connected tools.
The protocol boundary through which the Twin and external AIs access context. No direct tool reads ever.
Prepares the canonical handoff contract and delivers approved work to the customer's own execution environment.
The Approval Center. No handoff package can be generated without an L7 approval token.

content.title: Customer Zero Verification Panel
content.introduction: Observable proof that the Knowledge Workspace, Adaptive Spine, and Continuity Bridge are assembling correctly.

content.content:
Each box maps to a concrete stage:

• Identity — tenant_spine_config (schema_version = v2)
• Connectors — connected_connectors (>0)
• Hydration — creamy jobs (completed)
• Spine — *_data partitions (>0 rows)
• Memory — org_memory (>0)
• Continuity — assembler (memories returned)
• Twin — activation gate (activated = true)

content.title: Customer Zero Runbook
content.introduction: 11-step operational playbook for a successful tenant onboarding and validation run.

content.content:
1. Create tenant
2. Complete profile
3. Verify Identity box turns green
4. Connect first connector
5. Trigger hydration / initial sync
6. Confirm Spine data partitions have rows
7. Promote first memories to org_memory
8. Verify Continuity assembler returns context
9. Activate Twin and confirm proposals
10. Perform end-to-end approval + handoff test
11. Capture full green panel + evidence
